projectDescription: A web-based tool that converts images into string art by computing an optimal sequence of pin-to-pin thread paths on a circular hoop.
keyFeatures: Browser-based image-to-string-art conversion with no server required
Configurable number of pins, lines, and line weight
Greedy line selection algorithm using OpenCV and NumJS for high-quality output
Pin sequence export for reproducing string art physically
Step-by-step creation assistant with keyboard navigation
Three built-in example sequences for quick preview
Real-time preview updated every 10 lines during generation
publicApiSurface: N_PINS — number of pins evenly distributed around the circular hoop (default: 288)
MAX_LINES — maximum number of string segments to draw (default: 4000)
LINE_WEIGHT — darkness subtracted per line during error calculation (default: 20)
HOOP_DIAMETER — physical diameter of the hoop in meters used for thread-length estimation (default: 0.625)
NonBlockingCalculatePins() — calculates evenly spaced pin coordinates on a circle
NonBlockingPrecalculateLines() — precomputes pixel paths between every pin pair
NonBlockingLineCalculator() — greedily selects the best next pin to minimise image error
startCreating() — launches the step-by-step physical creation assistant
startDrawing() — auto-draws the full sequence on canvas
nextStep() / lastStep() — advances or reverses one string step in the assistant
